finding: Redirects table has no edit or delete control — created redirects are permanent from the UI.

evidence.quote: Redirects table has no delete/edit controls — created redirects cannot be removed from this UI.
Table columns OLD PATH / DESTINATION / STATUS / SOURCE / CREATED with no actions column

persona_rationale: A redirect manager I can't edit or undo from is not a redirect manager — every competitor lets me fix a typo'd 301; here one mistake is permanent and I'd never trust it for a site migration.

suggested_fix.current: Table columns end at "Created" with no action controls
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a per-row Edit/Delete action column (with confirm on delete) like Yoast Premium / Rank Math redirect managers

finding: Publish blockers are revealed one at a time in serialized internal phrasing, and the named field (CTA destination URL) lives only in a block-settings modal, not on the canvas.

evidence.quote: Fix content 1 · Destination URL" — the destination field is NOT on the canvas; it lives in Block actions → Edit settings modal
CTA destination URL field present only inside the Block settings modal

persona_rationale: In Webflow I get a publish-readiness panel listing everything at once with jump links; here I fixed one blocker only to discover another, then hunted the canvas for a field that lives in a modal. That's minutes of lost time per page.

suggested_fix.current: One-at-a-time cryptic chip e.g. "Fix content 1 · Destination URL"
suggested_fix.proposed: Show a complete plain-English readiness checklist with deep-links that open the exact field/modal, like Yoast's checklist and Webflow's pre-publish panel

finding: After the "Changes published" toast, the inline confirm-publish card stays open and re-prompts to publish again; no "Open live page" link is offered.

evidence.quote: "Changes published." toast appears BUT the confirm card remains open asking to publish again ("This will replace the current live version…").
Confirm publish card still visible after publish

persona_rationale: Every competitor closes the publish dialog and shows me a "View live" link on success. Here I couldn't tell if my second look at the card meant it failed, and there was no live-page link — I had to type the URL by hand.

suggested_fix.current: Confirm card persists and re-prompts after a successful publish
suggested_fix.proposed: Dismiss the card on success and show a success state with an "Open live page" link

finding: Scheduled-publish datetime field clears after save with no "Scheduled for…" confirmation anywhere in the editor.

evidence.quote: the field re-rendered EMPTY and no "Scheduled for …" status appeared anywhere in the editor
Empty dd/mm/yyyy datetime field, status still shows draft

persona_rationale: WordPress and Webflow both surface the scheduled time prominently; here I had no idea whether my schedule saved, which for a launch-day publish is unacceptable — I'd have to babysit it manually.

suggested_fix.current: Field empties after save with no scheduled-state confirmation
suggested_fix.proposed: Persist the chosen datetime in the field and show "Scheduled for {date/time}" on the Publish control and in the pages-list Status column, like WordPress

finding: Schedule timezone is hardcoded to Pacific Time for an Australian business, with no timezone control.

evidence.quote: Uses Pacific Time (America/Los_Angeles)
Uses Pacific Time (America/Los_Angeles). Leave blank unless this page should publish later.

persona_rationale: WordPress schedules in the site's configured timezone. Asking an Adelaide operator to mentally convert their launch time to LA time is exactly the kind of friction that makes me distrust a tool's localisation.

suggested_fix.current: Fixed "Uses Pacific Time (America/Los_Angeles)" with no control
suggested_fix.proposed: Respect the site/business timezone (or add a timezone selector) and label accordingly, e.g. "Publishes at 12:00pm ACST"

finding: No unsaved-changes warning when leaving the editor — typed content is silently lost.

evidence.quote: Leaving unsaved editor: navigated straight to /admin/pages with NO unsaved-changes warning — typed title silently lost
Returned to pages list after leaving unsaved editor

persona_rationale: Every editor I've used in two years warns me before discarding work. Losing a typed page because I clicked "Pages" is the kind of thing that makes me close the tab and not come back.

suggested_fix.current: Navigating away from an unsaved editor proceeds silently
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a "You have unsaved changes — leave anyway?" guard like Webflow / WordPress / Framer

finding: Revision history does not exist until the page is first published — no page-level undo while drafting.

evidence.quote: revisions do NOT exist while drafting. Empty state: "Revisions appear after publishing, library refreshes, or draft restores."
Revisions empty state shown before first publish

persona_rationale: WordPress keeps revisions of drafts I've never published; Webflow has backups. Here if I rework a draft before first publish and want the earlier version back, I'm stuck — that's a real regression from what I rely on.

suggested_fix.current: No restore points exist before first publish
suggested_fix.proposed: Autosave draft revisions from the first save, as WordPress (autosave + revisions) and Framer (version history) do

finding: Duplicate does not preserve or derive the source slug — it generates a random "draft-{hash}" slug.

evidence.quote: "Duplicate page" (confirm dialog) created "Copy of {title}" with auto slug "draft-4020bd0f" (original slug not preserved/derived)

persona_rationale: When I duplicate in WordPress I get a predictable "-copy" slug; a random hash means I have to re-slug every duplicate by hand, which defeats the point of duplicating a near-identical SEO page.

suggested_fix.proposed: Derive the slug from the title/source, e.g. "copy-of-{slug}", matching WordPress's "{slug}-copy" convention

finding: No bulk-select — archive and other row actions are per-row only.

evidence.quote: Archiving is per-row only; no bulk select.
Single-row ⋮ menu: Edit page / View live page / Duplicate page / Move to draft / Archive page

persona_rationale: WordPress's bulk-actions are muscle memory for me — archiving 10 stale pages one menu at a time is exactly the productivity tax that would make switching feel like a downgrade.

suggested_fix.current: Per-row ⋮ menu only, no checkbox column
suggested_fix.proposed: Add row checkboxes + a bulk action bar (Archive / Move to draft) like WordPress's bulk-actions dropdown and Webflow's multi-select

finding: Readiness and Status columns are colour-coded dots with no visible legend in the list view.

evidence.quote: READINESS and STATUS columns rendered as coloured dots only
button "SEO readiness: Needs work" / button "Page status: Published" (labels only in hover/accessible name)

persona_rationale: I shouldn't have to hover every dot to learn that red=blocked and amber=draft; WordPress just writes "Draft" / "Published" in the column. At a glance I can't triage which pages need work.

suggested_fix.current: Bare coloured dots with hover-only meaning
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a text label or status pill (e.g. "Draft" / "Published") in the column, like WordPress's status text and Webflow's status badges

finding: Editor block labels carry a prohibited ARIA attribute (serious axe violation).

evidence.quote: id "aria-prohibited-attr", impact "serious", selectors span[aria-label="Hero block"], span[aria-label="FAQ block"], count 2

persona_rationale: A serious a11y violation on the core editing surface signals the tool isn't held to the same bar as the mature CMS I'd be leaving; it makes me doubt overall build quality.

suggested_fix.current: aria-label on a span whose role prohibits it
suggested_fix.proposed: Move the accessible name onto the labelled control, or wrap in an element whose role permits aria-label

finding: "Template" starting point offers "No templates created" — a dead-end card for a first-time user.

evidence.quote: a "Template" option exists with "No templates created" (dead-end card for a first-time user)

persona_rationale: Offering me a template path that leads nowhere on day one is the kind of unfinished edge Webflow polished years ago; small, but it sets a "not quite done" tone.

suggested_fix.current: Empty Template option presented as a selectable card
suggested_fix.proposed: Hide the option until templates exist, or make it a "Save your first template" CTA, like Webflow only showing the gallery when populated

finding: New-page canvas is all greyed placeholder text with no guidance that fields must be filled before publishing.

evidence.quote: New page canvas is entirely placeholder ("Hero headline", "Hero body copy", "CTA label") with no guidance that these must be completed before publishing.
Editor opened with placeholder-only hero content

persona_rationale: Webflow eases a new user in; here I'm staring at grey text guessing which are required, then getting blindsided by publish blockers later. A small nudge up front would prevent the whack-a-mole downstream.

suggested_fix.proposed: Add a first-run hint or checklist ("Fill the hero headline to get started"), like Webflow's onboarding overlays
